assertions.name: created_rvv_patches_dir
assertions.text: Created rvv-patches directory for the operator
assertions.evidence: No rvv-patches directory exists under applications/llama.cpp/. All output files (rvv_gemv_q5_0_q8_0.inl, test.cpp, patch.diff, README.md) are placed directly in the outputs/ directory without the expected rvv-patches/*/ subdirectory structure.

assertions.name: created_inl_file
assertions.evidence: File rvv_gemv_q5_0_q8_0.inl exists but NOT under the expected path rvv-patches/*/rvv_*.inl under applications/llama.cpp/. It is in the flat outputs/ directory without the rvv-patches wrapper.

assertions.name: inl_has_rvv_guard
assertions.evidence: The .inl file contains '#if defined(__riscv_v_intrinsic)' on line 51 and '#endif // __riscv_v_intrinsic' on line 270, properly guarding the entire RVV implementation.

assertions.name: inl_has_rvv_header
assertions.evidence: The .inl file contains '#include <riscv_vector.h>' on line 52, inside the __riscv_v_intrinsic guard.

assertions.name: created_test_cpp
assertions.evidence: File test.cpp exists with comprehensive correctness tests, but it is NOT under the expected path rvv-patches/*/test.cpp under applications/llama.cpp/. It is in the flat outputs/ directory.

assertions.name: test_has_scalar_ref
assertions.evidence: test.cpp contains the function 'ggml_gemv_q5_0_q8_0_generic' (lines 124-185), a complete scalar reference implementation that dequantizes Q5_0 values and computes dot products element-by-element.

assertions.name: test_has_rng
assertions.evidence: test.cpp contains an LCG-based pseudo-random generator with fixed seed (rng_state = 42 on line 195, rng_next() on lines 197-199). The init_test_block function resets the seed for reproducibility.

assertions.name: created_patch_diff
assertions.evidence: File patch.diff exists with integration content, but it is NOT under the expected path rvv-patches/*/patch.diff under applications/llama.cpp/. It is in the flat outputs/ directory.

assertions.name: patch_targets_riscv
assertions.text: patch.diff targets the RISC-V arch source file
assertions.evidence: patch.diff references 'ggml/src/ggml-cpu/arch/riscv/repack.cpp' (lines 27-28), clearly targeting the RISC-V specific source file.

assertions.name: created_readme
assertions.evidence: File README.md exists with detailed documentation, but it is NOT under the expected path rvv-patches/*/README.md under applications/llama.cpp/. It is in the flat outputs/ directory.

assertions.name: readme_has_signature
assertions.evidence: README.md contains the full function signature 'void ggml_gemv_q5_0_q8_0(int n, float * GGML_RESTRICT s, size_t bs, const void * GGML_RESTRICT vx, const void * GGML_RESTRICT vy, int nr, int nc);' under the '## Function Signature' section (lines 31-34).

assertions.name: cross_platform_analysis
assertions.evidence: README.md contains a 'Cross-Platform Analysis' section (lines 172-177) comparing RISC-V RVV, ARM NEON, x86 AVX2, and Generic C approaches with file references and technique descriptions. The .inl file also references ARM NEON and x86 AVX2 implementations in its header comments (lines 34-36).

assertions.name: mentions_gap_analysis
assertions.evidence: No output file mentions 'gap analysis', 'rvv-gap-analysis', or 'cross-platform gap'. The README.md has a 'Cross-Platform Analysis' section but does not describe a gap analysis step.

assertions.name: test_uses_optnone
assertions.evidence: test.cpp uses '__attribute__((optnone))' on the scalar reference function ggml_gemv_q5_0_q8_0_generic (line 122) with comment 'Disable optimization to avoid LLVM bug'. It is also used on run_test (line 248).
